issues.description: The caption parenthetical lists 'predation, parasitism, commensalism' as the asymmetric interactions whose name is column-order-independent, but herbivory is also asymmetric and belongs in that set; only competition and mutualism are symmetric (−/− and +/+).
issues.suggested_fix: Include herbivory in the asymmetric list, e.g. 'For the asymmetric interactions (predation, herbivory, parasitism, commensalism)...'

issues.description: Müllerian mimicry example says 'multiple unpalatable / defended species sharing the same warning pattern,' while the compare slide (09) and pause answer (11) correctly emphasize predator learning. The example bullet is correct but thinner than the body — wording consistency only, not an error.
issues.suggested_fix: Optionally append '— so a predator learns the shared signal once' to mirror sections 09/11; not required for accuracy.

overall_assessment: All round-1 fixes landed and are factually sound. Six interaction signs are correct (competition −/−; predation/herbivory/parasitism +/−; mutualism +/+; commensalism +/0). Gause competitive exclusion and resource partitioning (MacArthur's warblers framed as partitioning, not exclusion) are accurate. Fundamental vs realized niche now correctly names competition and predation as the shrinking agents and states the realized niche is a subset that interactions shrink, never expand. Lotka-Volterra with Canada lynx/snowshoe hare ~10-year cycles uses no invented timeframes and appropriately hedges that real cycles involve more than two species. Batesian (palatable mimic of an unpalatable/defended model, effectively parasitic) vs Müllerian (multiple defended species share a warning pattern, effectively mutualistic) is correct, and the viceroy/monarch 'later reconsidered' caveat is preserved. Keystone is defined by disproportionate impact relative to abundance, clearly distinguished from trophic cascade as mechanism vs designation, and notes keystones can be mutualists or ecosystem engineers. Primary (bare rock, no soil, lichens/mosses, slow) vs secondary (soil remains, faster) succession is correct with soil as the dividing line. Richness vs evenness vs Shannon H is accurate, and the diversity→stability claim is properly hedged as correlation, not guarantee. Two minor wording items (herbivory omitted from one asymmetric-interaction list; a Müllerian example bullet thinner than the body) are non-blocking. No critical errors.
